en: I don't care what anyone tells you about our immigration policy,.
ko: 이민 정책에 대해 뭐라 하든 상관없는데,

en: It's very difficult to get in this country.
ko: 이 나라에 들어오는 거 진짜 어려워.

en: It's not like a broken condom where you're like,
I'm in, you don't just...
Americans hit their kids on the arm and bruise their body.
ko: 미국인들은 아이 팔을 때리고 멍들게 해.

en: Immigrants slap you across the face and bruise your soul.
ko: 이민자들은 뺨을 때리고 영혼에 멍들게 해.

en: It's Guantanamo of the mind.
We were in AP Calc together, but...
we had chemistry.
That's basically the way our parents are.
Ages zero through 30, they're like, no girls, right?
Then when you're 35, they're like, why can't you talk to girls?
ko: 35세 되면, 왜 여자한테 말도 못 해?

en: It's basically like, ah, why don't you kill me?
And my dad's from that generation like a lot of immigrants.
Where he feels like, if you come to this country,.
ko: 이 나라에 오면 인종차별 좀 겪을 거다,

en: You're gonna endure some racism.
But for me, like a lot of us, I was born here.
So I actually have the audacity of equality.
ko: 그래서 진짜로 평등에 대한 뻔뻔함이 있어.

en: Like, I'm like, no, I'm in honors gov, I have it right here.
ko: 아니, 난 AP 정치학 들었는데, 여기 있잖아.

en: Life, liberty, pursuit of happiness,.
Home and your community, all of it.
It says it right here, on YouTube.
